title: Computer Science & Engineering
intro: A practical roadmap for students interested in software engineering, web development, data science, and product-building careers.

stages.title: Build the foundation
details: Learn programming basics with C, C++, or Python.
Understand problem solving, algorithms, and version control.
Start basic HTML, CSS, and JavaScript projects.

stages.title: Grow your technical toolkit
details: Study data structures, OOP, databases, and operating systems.
Build CRUD projects with frontend and backend technologies.
Join programming contests, clubs, or hackathons.

stages.title: Specialize and gain experience
details: Choose a track like web, app, AI, cybersecurity, or data science.
Create portfolio projects and deploy them online.
Apply for internships and contribute to team projects.

stages.title: Prepare for industry
details: Practice interview questions, system design, and resume writing.
Strengthen GitHub, LinkedIn, and final year project quality.
Target junior developer, QA, data analyst, or support engineer roles.

title: Business Administration
intro: A structured roadmap for students aiming for careers in management, marketing, finance, entrepreneurship, and business analytics.

stages.title: Understand business basics
details: Learn accounting, principles of management, and business communication.
Improve presentation, writing, and teamwork skills.
Explore business case studies and student organizations.

stages.title: Develop analytical thinking
details: Study marketing, finance, HR, and organizational behavior.
Use spreadsheets for data analysis and reporting.
Take part in case competitions and business simulations.

stages.title: Choose a career path
details: Focus on finance, HR, marketing, supply chain, or entrepreneurship.
Build internship experience in relevant industries.
Learn reporting tools and market research techniques.

stages.title: Get career ready
details: Prepare a polished CV, LinkedIn, and interview answers.
Work on capstone projects and business presentations.
Apply for management trainee, analyst, coordinator, or executive roles.

title: Electrical & Electronic Engineering
intro: A roadmap for students who want careers in power systems, embedded systems, telecom, automation, or industrial engineering.

stages.title: Strengthen core science
details: Focus on mathematics, physics, circuits, and electrical fundamentals.
Learn basic circuit analysis and laboratory safety.
Document experiments and technical observations carefully.

stages.title: Build technical competence
details: Study electronics, signals, machines, and digital logic.
Practice simulation software and breadboard prototyping.
Work on simple IoT and automation mini-projects.

stages.title: Explore specialization
details: Choose among power, control systems, embedded systems, telecom, or robotics.
Take industry visits, technical workshops, and certifications.
Create project demos using sensors, controllers, or communication modules.

stages.title: Prepare for professional roles
details: Develop a strong final year project with real-world application.
Practice viva, technical interviews, and report writing.
Apply for trainee engineer, field engineer, or automation roles.

title: English & Communication
intro: A roadmap for students who want careers in writing, teaching, media, communication, content strategy, and research.

stages.title: Build language mastery
details: Improve grammar, composition, reading comprehension, and public speaking.
Read literature, essays, and academic texts regularly.
Start a writing habit through blogs, journals, or reviews.

stages.title: Practice communication professionally
details: Study linguistics, creative writing, and rhetoric.
Work on presentation, editing, and critical analysis.
Participate in debate clubs, publications, or event hosting.

stages.title: Choose your direction
details: Explore teaching, research, journalism, content writing, or public relations.
Build writing samples and communication portfolios.
Take internships in schools, agencies, media houses, or editorial teams.

stages.title: Launch your career
details: Prepare for teaching demos, writing tests, and interviews.
Refine your CV and create a digital portfolio of work.
Apply for roles in education, media, publishing, or corporate communication.
